patch.value: Cursor pagination and bounded-output defaults for list/search/context at scale

Bounded-output child of pm-9rxu: unbounded listings are token-catastrophic at 100k+ items. Related (not duplicate): pm-cfed lean JSON and pm-alnj search --limit shape single payloads; this defines the paging contract across read commands.

list/search/context accept a stable cursor (--after/--cursor per ADR contract) with deterministic ordering; at-scale default limits keep any single invocation under a documented token budget with an explicit truncation marker + next-cursor hint; small workspaces see no behavior change; contracts/MCP/completions/docs regenerated

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Wave-2 chain scoping 2026-07-10 (planning pass). This item is the sole blocker of pm-55ra (token-budget context packer) and thus gates the wave-2 tail; it is itself blocked by ADR pm-bl8x decision point 3 (cursor vs offset). Current surface (verified in code): pm list has --limit/--offset with NO default bound (list.ts:352-409, total emitted only when paginating); pm search has --limit only (search.ts:383/2608), no offset, no cursor; pm context defaults DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LIMIT=10 but --depth full lifts it to MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (context.ts:313/418) — the one truly unbounded default hole. No cursor token exists anywhere. Suggested slices: S1 cursor primitive in the SDK read/query layer shipped by pm-rjqr — opaque cursor encoding a stable sort tuple (sort key + id tiebreak), envelope gains next_cursor/has_more; this is ADR-input work and can start as a spike BEFORE pm-bl8x formally lands (recommend cursor-over-offset to the ADR, offset degrades at 100k+). S2 pm list adoption — threshold-gated default limit (small workspaces keep today's unbounded behavior per pm-z436 small-end concerns; large workspaces get a bounded default + next_cursor), --offset retained as compat alias. S3 pm search adoption — ranked-result cursors are rank-stability-sensitive (BM25/semantic scores shift between invocations); options: snapshot cursor (embed result-id window) vs re-rank-and-skip; decide in S3, not S1. S4 pm context/next bounded degradation — close the --depth full hole and hand off to pm-55ra, whose packer consumes S1 primitives for budget-aware truncation. Coordination: pm-9rxu (scale epic parent), pm-rjqr (S1 lands in its query facade), pm-3mna/pm-usfg promotion wave (S1 belongs in SDK not CLI per SDK-first), pm-z436 (threshold defaults), pm-gdi7/pm-nilh (envelope verbosity — next_cursor must not bloat lean output).

author: maintainer-agent
message: Link small-end scale + shipped SDK query facade (S1 fix site) per scoping note

author: maintainer-agent
